plantings.variety: Super Star + Yellow Sweet Spanish (~18 each)

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Pre-fruit feed — bone meal
tasks.description: Side-dress 1 tbsp bone meal per plant once first flowers appear. Supports fruit set and meets phosphorus demand during flowering.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
tasks.description: Check undersides of leaves on lower third of plant. Look for dark frass (droppings) as first sign. If hornworms found: spray Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) on affected foliage per label. Hand-pick any large worms already present. Reapply Bt after rain.

tasks.title: Mid-season feed — blood meal
tasks.description: Work 1 tbsp blood meal per plant into top inch of soil. Apply once heavy fruiting begins to compensate for nitrogen demand. Skip if foliage is already very dark green — excess nitrogen pushes leaf growth at expense of fruit.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Check potato_bags for frost recovery
tasks.description: Two sprouts frost-damaged May 23 (10 days ago); Wednesday's first 90°F day is the heat-stress test — confirm recovery before temps build to 91°F Friday.

tasks.title: First harvest check
tasks.description: Roma and paste types: firm and fully colored. Slicers: slight give when squeezed. Do not wait for perfect color in heat — fully vine-ripened tomatoes go soft fast in summer temperatures.

tasks.title: Check for suckers
tasks.description: Remove suckers forming in the crotch between main stem and lateral branches. Indeterminate varieties only (Amish Paste, Comstock, Bonny Best) — leave determinates (Roma, Orange Hat) unpruned. Pinch suckers when small (under 2 inches) for a cleaner break.

tasks.description: Look for brown spots with yellow halos on lower leaves. Remove affected leaves immediately and dispose (do not compost). Increase airflow by pruning dense interior foliage if severe.

tasks.title: Stop watering
tasks.description: Stop all irrigation 2 weeks before expected harvest. Dry conditions cure the outer wrapper and improve storage life significantly. Italian Red (softneck, W4) may be ready a week or two before Elephant & Music (hardneck, W3).

tasks.title: Stop watering
tasks.description: Stop all irrigation 2 weeks before expected harvest. Dry conditions cure the outer wrapper and improve storage life significantly. Italian Red (softneck, W4) may be ready a week or two before Elephant & Music (hardneck, W3).

tasks.title: Harvest check
tasks.description: Harvest when lower 3-4 leaves have browned but upper 5-6 are still green. Do not wait until all leaves are brown — the wrapper will deteriorate. Dig carefully with a fork, do not pull by the stem.

tasks.title: Harvest check
tasks.description: Harvest when lower 3-4 leaves have browned but upper 5-6 are still green. Do not wait until all leaves are brown — the wrapper will deteriorate. Dig carefully with a fork, do not pull by the stem.

tasks.title: Fall renovation
tasks.description: After last harvest in fall, cut all foliage back to 1 inch above crown. Remove old mulch and debris. Add 1 inch of fresh compost to the bed. Apply fall Berry-Tone feeding at label rate. This resets the plant for next year.

tasks.title: Early spring feed — Berry-Tone
tasks.description: Apply True Organic Berry-Tone at label rate (approximately 1/2 cup per 5 sq ft of bed) as soon as new growth appears in early spring. Work lightly into top inch of soil and water in well.

payload.headline: Heat peaks 97°F Saturday — protect beds before the gusts.
weather_notes: Secure e1–e4 tomato cage ties before Saturday — 33 mph gusts at 97°F can snap stems on 26-day-old transplants.
Water all beds early Saturday morning — 97°F arrives 2 days after today's Rachio run with zero rain in the forecast.
Skip supplemental watering on w2/w3 garlic this week — 97°F heat is accelerating curing and excess moisture at 41 days from harvest risks wrapper damage before the June 21 stop.
Check c2–c4 bean beds daily for emergence — day-7 seeds are mid-germination and 88–97°F heat with no rain can bake the seed zone dry.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due Sunday; 88–97°F heat through Saturday will surge lateral growth on this indeterminate variety — pinch before suckers pass 2 inches.

suggested_tasks.title: Hill remaining 3 potato_bags plants
suggested_tasks.description: Three of five sprouted plants still need hilling after the June 3 partial job — cover stems before heat-wave growth makes it harder to do cleanly.

suggested_tasks.title: Mulch e1–e4 tomato bases before Saturday
suggested_tasks.description: Apply 2-inch straw mulch around tomato bases before Saturday — bare soil under 97°F with 33 mph gusts will lose moisture rapidly.

payload.headline: Blight check due tomorrow — 91°F heat by Friday
weather_notes: Water c2–c4 bean beds and potato_bags Thursday — 90°F Wednesday burns off today's irrigation from the shallow seedling zone by day 2 with zero rain in the 7-day forecast.
Water e1–e4 tomatoes deeply Thursday — 89–91°F heat builds through Friday with no rain; Thursday is the 2-day rewater window after today's Rachio run.
Hold manual watering on w2/w3 garlic now — heat wave is accelerating natural dry-down at 43 days to harvest; complete stop due June 21.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow; inspect lower leaves before 90°F Wednesday heat accelerates any latent infection into rapid spread.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: One day overdue with bed freshly irrigated today — ideal window to apply fish emulsion before 90°F heat reduces nutrient uptake.

suggested_tasks.title: Check potato_bags for frost recovery
suggested_tasks.description: Two sprouts frost-damaged May 23 (10 days ago); Wednesday's first 90°F day is the heat-stress test — confirm recovery before temps build to 91°F Friday.

payload.headline: Two tasks due today — 90°F heat arrives Thursday
weather_notes: Water c2/c3/c4 bean beds today — seeds sown 4 days ago are mid-germination and 85°F Tuesday will dry surface soil before sprouts emerge.
Water potato_bags before Tuesday — bag soil dries twice as fast as raised beds; 3 days since last irrigation and 90°F arrives Thursday.
Water e1–e4 tomatoes deeply today — four consecutive 85–90°F days begin tomorrow with zero rain in the 7-day forecast.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today with strawberries at 37 days actively fruiting — apply on moist soil before Wednesday's 89°F dries the bed.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check blackberry for flower clusters
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today in year 2 Triple Crown's first fruiting season — confirming flower set now determines whether the plant yields berries this summer.

suggested_tasks.title: Check Amish Paste e1 for suckers
suggested_tasks.description: Amish Paste is indeterminate at 23 days — Wednesday's 89°F will surge lateral growth; pinch suckers now before they exceed 2 inches.

payload.headline: Hornworm check today, blackberry tomorrow, 85°F Tuesday
weather_notes: Hold all watering today — Rachio ran 2 days ago at 75°F with zero rain risk; every active bed is at adequate moisture.
Water potato_bags and c2–c4 bean beds by Tuesday — 85°F heat arrives with no rain in the 7-day forecast; bag soil and just-sown bean seeds lose moisture faster than established raised beds.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — tomato beds are 22 days in and entering the peak hornworm window; today's warm sunny conditions are ideal for checking lower leaves for frass before the week heats up.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check blackberry for flower clusters
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow — Triple Crown is entering its first fruiting season at year 2 and confirming flowers now sets expectations for summer berry yield.

suggested_tasks.title: Check frost-damaged potato sprouts for recovery
suggested_tasks.description: Two sprouts were knocked back by frost May 23 — 8 days later with 85°F arriving Tuesday, confirm recovery or spot-replant before soil temperatures spike.

payload.headline: Hornworms due today — strawberry feed and blackberry check tomorrow
weather_notes: Water all beds Monday — no rain forecast all week and 85°F arrives Tuesday with soil already 3 days dry after May 29 irrigation.
Keep c2–c4 bean beds surface-moist daily — seeds sown May 28 are mid-germination and 85°F Tuesday will dry surface soil before sprouts emerge.
Check and water potato_bags Monday — bag soil dries twice as fast as raised beds and Tuesday's 85°F hits 4 days after last irrigation.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for hornworms — apply Bt if found
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today on 22-day tomatoes; today's clear dry conditions are ideal for spotting frass before Tuesday's 85°F accelerates pest activity.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check blackberry for flower clusters
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow at year 2 Triple Crown's first fruiting window — confirming flowers are set now determines whether the plant yields this season.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow with strawberries at 36 days in active fruiting — apply on Monday's moist soil before the week's heat builds.

payload.headline: Check Amish Paste suckers Sunday before 83°F Monday
weather_notes: Skip all irrigation through Sunday — beds watered May 29 and today's 70% rain leaves every active bed saturated.
Inspect e1–e4 tomato lower leaves for early blight Sunday — today's 76% humidity and active rain are prime blight-spreading conditions.
Check c2, c3, c4 bean beds Monday — seeds sown May 28 can stall if surface soil bakes dry under 83°F heat; water if dry.

surfaced_tasks.title: Check for suckers
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due tomorrow on Amish Paste (indeterminate, e1) — Sunday's clear dry window after today's rain is ideal to pinch lateral shoots before Monday's 83°F accelerates growth.

surfaced_tasks.title: Inspect for early blight
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due June 3 and today's 76% humidity with active rain creates prime blight conditions — inspect Sunday while leaves are drying before Monday's heat locks in any infection.

suggested_tasks.title: Check blackberry for flower clusters
suggested_tasks.description: Triple Crown enters its first fruiting season (year 2, planted May 2025) — confirm second-year canes are flowering now, the peak window for Zone 7b.

payload.headline: Tie down tomatoes before 3pm — T-storms through Saturday
weather_notes: Secure e1–e4 cage ties and stakes before 3pm — T-storms arrive this afternoon on 20-day-old transplants with 60%+ rain continuing overnight through Saturday.
Skip all irrigation through Sunday — tonight's 63% and Saturday's 61% rain probability will fully recharge every active bed.
Pull back straw mulch edges on w2–w3 garlic before tonight's rain — 47 days from July harvest, excess weekend moisture risks bulb rot in the final curing stage.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due in 3 days (June 1) with strawberries at 34 days actively fruiting — Sunday's post-rain moist soil is ideal timing for this liquid application.

suggested_tasks.title: Check peach for standing water after rain
suggested_tasks.description: Tree logged struggling May 23 and May 28 — heavy rain tonight through Saturday risks waterlogging an already-stressed root zone; check for pooling at the base on Sunday.

payload.headline: Deep-water peach today before Friday thunderstorms arrive
weather_notes: Water potato_bags today — 87°F heat with 3 days since last irrigation drains bag soil faster than raised beds, and Friday's 33% storm chance is not a reliable substitute.
Hold irrigation after today — Friday night through Saturday brings 49–58% rain probability that should recharge all beds.
Check and tighten e1–e4 tomato cage ties before Friday — gusty thunderstorms arrive Friday afternoon on 19-day-old transplants.

surfaced_tasks.title: Deep water the peach tree Thursday
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today and logged struggling May 23 — 87°F with 3 dry days is peak stress window before Friday rain breaks the drought.

payload.headline: Peas 8 days from target — heat toughens pods fast
weather_notes: Secure e1–e4 tomato cages before noon — 32 mph gusts arriving this afternoon with 40% storm chance.
Pick c2–c4 peas no later than Wednesday — 85°F heat through Thursday toughens pods fast with harvest just 8 days out.
Water potato_bags Thursday — two consecutive 85°F days after yesterday's irrigation will drain bag soil by then.

suggested_tasks.title: Pick c2–c4 peas before Wednesday heat
suggested_tasks.description: Pods forming heavily since May 12; harvest target June 3 and 85°F heat Wed–Thu toughens unpicked pods daily.

suggested_tasks.title: Deep water the peach tree Thursday
suggested_tasks.description: Logged struggling May 23; two 85°F days hit before Thursday when Rachio's last run (May 25) will be 3 days old.

payload.headline: Peas 9 days out, trellis 3 days late
weather_notes: Stake blackberry and peach before Tuesday — wind gusts to 33 mph could uproot or snap 2-day-old transplants.
Provide afternoon shade for peach today — logged as struggling at planting and 89°F today is peak heat stress before roots can establish.
Pick c2–c4 peas before Tuesday's heat — 89°F today and 86°F tomorrow accelerate pod toughening with harvest just 9 days out.
Hold manual watering through Wednesday — Rachio already ran zones 1 and 2 this morning and Wednesday's 20% storm chance may cover any deficit.

surfaced_tasks.title: Install trellis in c1 bean bed
surfaced_tasks.reason: Three days overdue — Good Mother Stollard pole beans are 22 days old and today's 89°F heat surges vine growth daily without support.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due June 1 (7 days) and strawberries at 30 days post-plant are entering peak fruiting season when nitrogen matters most.

suggested_tasks.title: Pick ripe pods from c2–c4 peas
suggested_tasks.description: Pods formed heavily by May 12 and harvest is 9 days out — 89°F today will toughen unpicked pods fast.

suggested_tasks.title: Rig shade cloth over peach mid-afternoon
suggested_tasks.description: Logged as 'looking really rough' at planting 2 days ago — 89°F today risks sunscald and transplant failure before roots can establish.

payload.headline: Stake peach, trellis beans before Tuesday's 33 mph gusts
weather_notes: Stake the peach tree before Tuesday — 33 mph gusts forecast and it was already logged struggling at planting two days ago.
Check cages and ties on e1–e4 tomatoes before Tuesday — 33 mph gusts can snap stems on 16-day-old transplants.
Start picking c2–c4 peas today — 89°F heat with harvest 9 days out will toughen pods ahead of schedule.
Hold irrigation through Tuesday — watered today; reassess Wednesday when a 20% storm chance may bring natural moisture.

surfaced_tasks.title: Install trellis in c1 bean bed
surfaced_tasks.reason: Three days overdue — Good Mother Stollard pole beans are at day 22 and vining fast in 89°F heat.

surfaced_tasks.title: Mid-season feed — fish emulsion
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due June 1 in seven days — strawberries are 30 days in and actively fruiting; apply now to support continuous berry production.

payload.headline: Peas 10 days out — 89°F heat will toughen pods fast
weather_notes: Pick from c2–c4 peas before Tuesday — 86–89°F Memorial Day weekend toughens pods fast with harvest only 10 days away.
Water peach, blackberry, and potato_bags Monday — all three are under a week old; Memorial Day's 89°F hits 2 days after their last drink.
Check the peach for wilt today and tomorrow — logged as struggling yesterday; 86–89°F on a day-1 transplant warrants shade cloth if leaves droop.

surfaced_tasks.title: Install trellis in c1 bean bed
surfaced_tasks.reason: Overdue two days — Good Mother Stollard is at day 21 and actively reaching for support; 89°F Memorial Day weekend will surge vine growth before untethered vines can be cleanly redirected.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest peas before heat toughens pods
suggested_tasks.description: Pods were forming heavily in c2–c4 as of May 12 — 86–89°F Memorial Day weekend will toughen unpicked pods fast; c4 (Lillian's) had the most and should be checked first.

payload.headline: 89°F Memorial Day — trellis beans before they tangle
weather_notes: Water e1–e4 tomatoes deeply today — no rain forecast through Memorial Day and 83–89°F weekend heat will rapidly deplete bed moisture.
Check potato_bags moisture daily through Monday — 4-day Yukon Gold roots haven't established yet and bag soil dries twice as fast as beds in 83–89°F heat.
Pick from c2–c4 peas by Tuesday — 83–89°F heat this weekend accelerates pod fill and toughening, potentially pulling harvest ahead of the June 3 target.

surfaced_tasks.title: Install trellis in c1 bean bed
surfaced_tasks.reason: Overdue by 1 day and pole bean vines will accelerate fast through the 83–89°F weekend — tangling becomes hard to correct once vines outpace support.

suggested_tasks.title: Harvest check c4 peas before heat toughens pods
suggested_tasks.description: Lillian's Caseload had the most pods forming on May 12 — 89°F Memorial Day weekend will accelerate toughening and splitting; check and pick c4 first.

payload.headline: Scapes and trellis due today — hot dry weekend ahead
weather_notes: Water potato_bags before Saturday — freshly planted Yukon Gold (day 3) in bags dry out twice as fast as raised beds during 83-88°F weekend with zero rain through Memorial Day.
Water c2-c4 peas before Sunday — pods at fill-stage lose quality under heat stress without consistent soil moisture; 87-88°F Sunday-Monday with zero rain forecast.

surfaced_tasks.title: Snap scapes on w3 Italian Red garlic
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today at peak scape window — every day delayed redirects energy away from bulb development ahead of the July harvest.

surfaced_tasks.title: Install trellis in c1 bean bed
surfaced_tasks.reason: Due today — Good Mother Stollard pole beans need vertical support now before the warm weekend accelerates vine growth and tangling.
